paper_title: Lake Mondsee Sediment and Isotope Analysis

methods.description: Monitoring of sediment flux and stable isotope composition using moored sediment traps to understand seasonal variations and detrital contributions.

steps.description: The sediments were monitored with two moored sediment traps at a 3 d time resolution from January 2011 to November 2013 (proximal trap) and from April 2012 to November 2013 (distal trap).
steps.searchable_summary: monitor sediment flux using moored sediment traps

steps.description: Stable oxygen and carbon values were determined for all samples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stable oxygen and carbon isotopes in sediment samples

steps.description: We calculated equilibrium δ 18 O for calcite precipitation from lake water and compared these values with measured δ 18 O from trap samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and compare equilibrium δ 18 O for calcite precipitation

methods.description: Analysis of stable isotope composition in sediment cores to identify detrital layers and assess historical flood events.

steps.description: We measured the stable isotope composition of discrete seasonal calcite layers, detrital layers, and bulk sediment samples in a surface sediment core for the time interval from 1988 to 2013 CE.
steps.searchable_summary: measure stable isotope composition in sediment core layers

steps.description: The detrital contribution in stable isotope data was estimated from the maximum difference from previous and following samples.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate detrital contribution from isotope data differences

methods.description: Comparison of stable isotope data with flood time series to evaluate the potential of isotopes as proxies for historical flood events.

steps.description: We compare the isotope data with instrumental flood time series to further test the potential of stable isotopes as paleoflood proxy.
steps.searchable_summary: compare isotope data with flood time series

steps.description: We define two empiric thresholds for δ 18 O deviations, >0.5‰ and >2.3‰, to reconstruct flood magnitudes.
steps.searchable_summary: define thresholds for δ 18 O deviations to reconstruct flood magnitudes
